  "executive_summary": {
    "one_sentence_positioning": "DEKSDEN notes is a deep practitioner of AI-assisted software engineering, specializing in CLI-based agentic workflows, context engineering (Memory Banks), and rigorous model evaluations.",
    "strongest_system_value": "Provides highly detailed, battle-tested methodologies for structuring AI SWE projects (DeksdenFlow, Memory Banks) and empirical evaluations of frontier and open-source models.",
    "weakest_system_value": "Highly opinionated towards CLI agents and specific custom workflows, which may not align with users seeking out-of-the-box IDE solutions or beginner-friendly tutorials.",
    "best_fit_queries": [
      "How to structure a Memory Bank for an AI agent?",
      "What is the best workflow for parallel agent execution?",
      "How do frontier models compare in code review and planning tasks?",
      "How to manage context limits in long-running agent tasks?"
    ],
    "poor_fit_queries": [
      "How to use Cursor or Windsurf for beginners?",
      "Basic prompt engineering for ChatGPT web interface.",
      "How to write code manually without AI."
    ],
    "confidence": 0.95
  },

      "Vendor benchmarks are often marketing; real-world evals are the only source of truth."
    ],
    "distinctive_angle": "Treats AI agents as autonomous workers requiring strict management, isolated environments (git worktrees), and structured memory (Memory Banks), rather than just smart autocomplete tools.",

  "practical_patterns": [
    {
      "pattern": "DeksdenFlow: Use Git worktrees to isolate parallel agent tasks, preventing them from corrupting the main branch or interfering with each other.",
      "when_to_use": "When orchestrating multiple autonomous agents to work on different features of the same codebase simultaneously.",
      "failure_modes": [
        "Merge conflicts when integrating worktrees back into the main branch (requires a dedicated merge queue/agent)."
      ],
      "evidence_refs": [

        "P0183"
      ]
    },
    {
      "pattern": "Task Folders (.tasks/): Create a dedicated folder for each agent task to store intermediate reports, checklists, and logs.",
      "when_to_use": "To save the orchestrator's context window by offloading detailed sub-agent outputs to the file system.",
      "failure_modes": [
        "Agents may fail to read the files if not explicitly prompted to do so."
      ],
      "evidence_refs": [
        "P0027",
        "P0028"
      ]
    },
    {
      "pattern": "Atomic Duo Files: Split documentation into an architecture file (WHAT and WHY) and a guide file (HOW).",
      "when_to_use": "When structuring a Memory Bank to allow agents to load only the high-level context unless implementation details are specifically needed.",
      "failure_modes": [
        "Over-fragmentation of documentation making it hard to maintain."
      ],
      "evidence_refs": [
        "P0040"
      ]
    }
